name: Andorhal
region: Eastweald in northern Lordaeron, the kingdom's grain-distribution hub

sensoryProfile.sight: Open gates, four granary towers, wagon beds axle to axle, loading yards, stamp boards, bakehouse fronts, and ordinary market traffic moving under winter smoke.
sensoryProfile.sound: Hooves on stone, manifests being stamped, market quarrels over bread weight, loading crews cursing at receipt slates, and later the muffled night quiet of a district still working after dark.
sensoryProfile.smell: Yeast, scorched flour, grain dust, lamp oil, old paper, and the colder lane-stink of damp chaff and banked ovens.
sensoryProfile.touch: Mud striped with chaff, stone floors glazed with flour, and the broad pressure of corruption riding the city even while its work stays ordinary.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: A functioning logistical machine whose normalcy is the horror: the city feeds people by day while hiding disciplined cult work inside the same routine.

establishedFacts.fact: Andorhal is now the next route objective once the village evidence proves the plague is moving through grain and storehouses.
Jaina imagines it not as skyline but as loading yards, clerks, manifests, and enough grain infrastructure to drown a kingdom by ledger.
The party believes the supply-chain answer will be found farther southwest along the road toward Andorhal rather than in one more village symptom.
The grain trail now points to Andorhal through multiple feeder roads rather than one direct village-to-city line, making the city a convergence basin instead of a single obvious road-end.
The direct approach may already be too exposed or too scrubbed; the southwest branch near Strahnbrad looks like the better way to reach Andorhal from the Barov side of the road web.
Andorhal is still visibly alive and busy when the party reaches it, with grain traffic, bakery work, and loading yards operating in plain sight.
The city's corruption is strong enough to press across Jaina's heavier wards as a broad ambient field before entry.
The Strahnbrad road, western feeder, and main southeast approach all knit into the final run toward the city, making the route structure explicit on-page.
Andorhal's officials, guards, and clerks still think in seals, paperwork, and routine inspections, which lets a strong cover story pass them more easily than open alarm would.
The city contains both an inspection bureaucracy and a granary district sophisticated enough to hide necromantic contamination inside ordinary packaging work.
Corin's office, Havel's district maps, and Jaina's sample read prove the city has been generating ignored warnings from both institutional and street levels for months.

eventsHere.event: Andorhal is named as the new pursuit destination at the end of chapter4.
Chapter5 narrows the pursuit toward Andorhal through Barov-linked feeder roads and a southwest approach rather than a blind headlong ride.
The party reaches visual approach on a still-working Andorhal and commits to records, towers, and signatories as the next move.
The party enters under cover, links up with Corin and Havel, and proves the city is a live hub rather than a dead endpoint.
Jaina identifies packaging-stage contamination while Sylvanas maps the hidden local cell, turning Andorhal from suspicion into a same-night capture plan.
